justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). (2) Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out in escalations and edge cases — e.g., route recalculation depends on delivery state changes, vehicle class constraint spans Fleet and Route contexts. (3) Vehicle class rule correctly marked invariant:true (BR-1), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7), notification window correctly marked invariant:false (BR-6). (4) 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, far exceeding the minimum of 4. (5) All three route recalculation triggers enumerated (new delivery added, delivery failed, manual deviation) in BR-3/BR-4/BR-5.

observations.text: Excellent bounded context decomposition with 4 clearly named contexts and ownership descriptions
10 acceptance criteria covering happy path, edge cases, and cross-context scenarios (AC-6, AC-8 test vehicle class across contexts)
12 edge cases identified including recalculation storms, notification regression, and stranded deliveries — well beyond expectations
Rich escalations with multi-option recommendations, particularly the multi-driver zone ambiguity and retry semantics
Comprehensive ubiquitous language with 12 precisely defined terms

Route recalculation triggers (BR-3, BR-4, BR-5) marked invariant:false — the rubric expected invariant:true since the system must always recalculate on these events. Bird's justification (they're policy choices) is defensible but diverges from the expected answer
BR-9 (zone constraint) and BR-10 (pending-only) identified as additional invariants beyond what the rubric required — shows deeper domain analysis
Cross-context dependency between delivery lifecycle and route recalculation surfaced as an integration point in escalations, exactly as expected for MJ handoff
Business impact analysis identifies 6 stakeholder groups with nuanced positive/negative impact assessment

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out in business_context and throughout business rules. Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant:true (BR-2), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (BR-8), notification window correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, far exceeding the 4 minimum. All three recalculation triggers correctly enumerated (BR-4, BR-5, BR-6). Cross-context integration points surfaced in escalations for MJ-level architectural decisions.

observations.text: Excellent bounded context separation with clear ownership descriptions for each context
11 business rules identified with thorough invariant justifications — goes well beyond the minimum
10 acceptance criteria covering happy path, edge cases, concurrency, and idempotency (duplicate notification prevention)
Rich edge case analysis (10 scenarios) surfacing unspecified behaviors like multi-driver assignment and mid-delivery recalculation
Escalations are exceptionally well-structured with concrete options and recommendations — exactly what MJ needs for architectural decisions
Cross-context dependency between Delivery Lifecycle state changes and Route recalculation is explicitly identified
Correctly classified route recalculation triggers as invariant:false (policy decisions) rather than invariant:true

BR-3 (auto-generate route at shift start) marked invariant:false is defensible but debatable — the rubric expected recalculation triggers as invariant:true since the system 'must always recalculate on these events'. Bird chose to classify them as policy, which is a reasonable domain interpretation but diverges from rubric intent
Ubiquitous language section is comprehensive with 11 well-defined terms that would genuinely help align a development team
Confidence level of 52 is appropriately calibrated given the significant spec gaps identified

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out ('Smart Route Optimization feature is an orchestration across all four contexts'). Vehicle class rule correctly marked invariant:true (BR-1), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7), notification window correctly marked invariant:false (BR-6). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Route recalculation triggers correctly enumerated as 3 events (new delivery, failed delivery, driver deviation) in BR-3/4/5 and ubiquitous language.

observations.text: Identified 4 clearly named bounded contexts with precise ownership boundaries for each
Cross-context orchestration explicitly noted — 'Smart Route Optimization feature is an orchestration across all four contexts, not a single context'
Vehicle class constraint (BR-1) correctly classified as invariant:true with strong justification referencing physical and legal constraints
Stop duration (BR-7) and notification window (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant:false
10 acceptance criteria — double the minimum — covering happy path, vehicle class filtering, recalculation triggers, notifications, zone isolation, and concurrency
Went beyond requirements with 10 well-considered edge cases including concurrent recalculation, shift-end behavior, and zero-duration validation
4 escalations with concrete options and recommendations surface genuine spec gaps for MJ and stakeholders
Discovered additional invariants not in rubric (zone partitioning BR-8, no double-assignment BR-9, completed stops immutable BR-10) — shows deep domain reasoning

Route recalculation triggers (BR-3/4/5) marked invariant:false — rubric expected invariant:true since the system 'must always recalculate on these events'. Bird's justification (they're optimization policies, not data integrity constraints) is defensible but diverges from rubric expectation

The invariant:false classification for recalculation triggers, while diverging from rubric, includes well-reasoned justification that these are operational policies not data integrity constraints — a legitimate domain interpretation
Confidence level of 55 is well-calibrated given the number of genuine ambiguities identified in the spec
